properties.remark: MESONET STATION LMRM8, ANTELOPE RAWS.
MESONET STATION YLWM8, YELLOW MULE RAWS.

KRTV RELAYED A REPORT OF A BRIEF TORNADO NEAR MOORE. MULTIPLE PICTURES SHOW THE TORNADO LOFTED DIRT INTO THE AIR...BUT NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED. THE TORNADO WAS ASSOCIATED WITH A NON-SUPERCELL THUNDERSTORM THAT PASSED JUST NORTH OF MOORE.
VIDEO OF A WATER SHOOTING OUT OF A STORM DRAIN/SEWER AND FLOODING A ROAD. ANOTHER VIDEO OF FLOODING AT A LOCAL GROCERY STORE.
TRAINED SPOTTER ESTIMATED A WIND GUST OF 60-70 MPH NORTH OF HAVRE. WIND GUST WAS LIKELY CAUSED BY A DOWNDRAFT FROM A WEAKENING THUNDERSTORM. PEA SIZE HAIL WAS REPORTED AT SPOTTER'S LOCATION AS WELL.
SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER SIZE HAIL FALLING AT HIS LOCATION JUST EAST OF HAVRE. QUARTER SIZE HAIL WAS THE LARGEST HE COULD SEE SO FAR.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED 1.1 DIAMETER HAIL.
LINCOLN FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTS A LARGE PINE TREE DOWN (SNAPPED AT BASE). PEA SIZE HAIL AND 0.40 INCHES OF RAINFALL IN 30 MINUTES. REPORT VIA FACEBOOK. TIME/LOCATION ESTIMATED VIA RADAR.
79 MPH WIND GUST AT HILLDALE COLONY FARMS.
SPOTTER RELAYED DELAYED REPORT OF QUARTER-SIZE HAIL.

FLASH FLOOD WITH DEBRIS FLOW REPORTED IN THE STONEWALL SUBDIVISION ALONG STONEWALL CREEK NORTH-NORTHWEST OF LINCOLN. SOCIAL MEDIA REPORT WITH PHOTOS. TIME AND LOCATION ESTIMATED. RADAR INDICATES A STRONG THUNDERSTORM BETWEEN 8-9PM 5-25.

THIS IS A FOLLOW UP TO THE PREVIOUS REPORT OF NICKEL SIZE HAIL AT THIS SAME LOCATION. SPOTTER REPORTS THE HAIL HAS INCREASED IN SIZE AND IS NOW UP TO THE SIZE OF QUARTERS. NO WIND AT THIS TIME, JUST HAIL.
REPORT RELAYED THROUGH MEDIA WITH PICTURES OF HAIL NEAR AUGUSTA. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
SPOTTER ESTIMATED HAIL UP TO QUARTER SIZE OCCURRING AT THIS TIME.
CHASER/SPOTTER REPORTED QUARTER-SIZE HAIL IN GEYSER. SIZE WAS ESTIMATED.
PUBLIC REPORTED UP TO ONE INCH HAIL. MOSTLY DIMES TO NICKELS BUT A FEW QUARTERS NEAR COFFEE CREEK.
WATER ALSO RUNNING IN STREETS.
SPOTTER RELAYED THROUGH NWS EMPLOYEE WITH A PHOTO.
DIME TO QUARTER SIZE HAIL, LASTING FOR 20 MINUTES. 2.5 INCHES OF RAIN WITH THE STORM.
HEAVY RAIN PRODUCED STREET AND BASEMENT FLOODING IN DOWNTOWN LEWISTOWN.
NICKEL-SIZED HAIL AND VERY HEAVY RAIN ON US HIGHWAY 2 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 415 & 416.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS HAIL REPORT FROM 6 ESE LEWISTOWN. QUITE A BIT OF PEA TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL. WINDS 40 TO 50 MPH.
QUITE A BIT OF PEA TO QUARTER SIZED HAIL.
FACEBOOK POST OF A VIDEO OF A LOT OF HAIL, UP TO A QUARTER IN SIZE, FALLING 10 MILES SOUTHEAST OF LEWISTOWN.
ROOF RIPPED OFF BARN. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH OR HIGHER. DELAYED REPORT, TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
ROOF RIPPED OFF BARN. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH OR HIGHER. DELAYED REPORT...TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
HALF DOLLAR-SIZED HAIL. MULTIPLE REPORTS OF BROKEN WINDOWS ON NORTH SIDES OF BUILDINGS. DELAYED REPORT, TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

STORM CHASER VIDEO CONFIRMS TOUCHDOWN OF A NON-SUPERCELL TORNADO. TORNADO LASTED AROUND 5 MINUTES.
FACEBOOK VIDEO OF ALOT OF WATER OVER ROAD IN SHELBY NEAR THE INTERSTATE. WATER UP TO TRAIN TRACKS. SOME CARS STALLED IN THE ROAD.
GOOD SIZED COTTONWOOD TREE BRANCHES DOWN. ESTIMATED TO BE THE SIZE OF FENCE POSTS.
FACEBOOK REPORT OF DOWNED TREES, SMALL HAIL, AND NO ELECTRICITY SOUTH OF POWER. ALSO REPORTS 2 INCHES OF RAIN.
REPORTS A LOT OF WIND AND A LOT OF PEA SIZED HAIL. HAIL COVERING THE GROUND. POWER WENT OUT. WINDOW SCREENS DESTROYED WITH SOME MINOR GLASS DAMAGE. PAINT ON HOUSE SIDINGS ALSO DAMAGED.
FACEBOOK VIDEO OF WATER RUNNING ON ROADS, ALMOST TOUCHING THE LOWER DOOR PORTIONS OF A CAR.
MOSTLY SMALL HAIL WITH A FEW QUARTER-SIZED HAILSTONES MIXED IN.
HAIL UP TO QUARTER-SIZE.

TOWN HALL.
SOURDOUGH RIDGE.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL REPORTED IN NE BLAINE COUNTY. SEMI AND TRAILER ALSO REPORTED TO BE BLOWN OVER IN THE AREA.
61 MPH WIND GUST.
ESTIMATED WIND GUST NEAR 80 MPH AND HAIL UP TO QUARTER SIZE. SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO CROPS.
ESTIMATED WINDS OF 60 MPH. LOTS OF PEA SIZE HAIL AND RAIN.
ESTIMATED 65 MPH WINDS WITH LARGE TREES KNOCKED DOWN IN MULTIPLE LOCATIONS.
HAIL STARTED OUT LARGE THEN GOT SMALLER. ENOUGH SMALL HAIL TO COVER THE GROUND TO A DEPTH OF SEVERAL INCHES. CROPS RUINED. WINDS ESTIMATED AT 30 MPH.
DELAYED REPORT OF MULTIPLE TREES DOWN IN TOWN AND NO POWER. PEA SIZED HAIL. ESTIMATES 60 MPH WINDS.

POST-EVENT REPORT. LARGE HEALTHY CONIFER TREE DOWNED AT THE ROOTS IN HIGH WINDS. TRAINED SPOTTER SUBMITTED PHOTO TO SOCIAL MEDIA. TIME OF EVENT ESTIMATED.
DELAYED REPORT.
STRONG WINDS KNOCKED DOWN SOME TREE BRANCHES UP TO 33 INCHES IN LENGTH.
DOT SENSOR.

TORNADO TOOK OUT A SHED, KNOCKED DOWN 200 FEET OF BUCK AND RAIL FENCING, DESTROYED 150 FEET OF 3-WIRE FENCING WITH A T-POST BENT AT A 30-DEGREE ANGLE, BLEW SOME DEBRIS 0.5 MILE AWAY, AND DESTROYED A 50 POUND CAST IRON AND TILE DECK. TORNADO TRACK WAS ABOUT 0.75 MILE LONG. BASED ON DAMAGE RATED AN EF0 TORNADO.

FENCE BLOWN DOWN AND SHED DAMAGED. LOCATION IS ON THE BENCH NEAR STEELE CREEK. TIME OF OCCURRENCE IS ESTIMATED. SECOND HAND REPORT RELAYED FROM NWS MISSOULA BUT CORROBORATED BY BEAVERHEAD COUNTY DISPATCH AND DES.
MESONET STATION EKHM8, ELKHORN RAWS - 8 W TOWNSEND. TIME OF EVENT IS ESTIMATED.
TREE DAMAGE IN THE HAVRE AREA AS THE STORM MOVED THROUGH AROUND 7 PM.
